expectations.text: Script creates spline data element with data_element create spline
AKISPL function is used for the nonlinear X-direction force
force create direct general_force is used (NOT force create element_like bushing)
expectations.evidence: Script uses BOTH force create element_like bushing (rubber_mount_linear) for Y/Z translation and rotational stiffness AND force create direct general_force (rubber_mount_x_nonlinear) for X only — bushing is explicitly forbidden

expectations.text: Spline table covers -10mm to 10mm with (-10,-8000), (-5,-3000), (0,0), (5,3000), (10,8000) data points
Y and Z force functions are linear (5000 * DY or 5000 * DZ, or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Bushing stiffness = 0.0, 5000.0, 5000.0 provides 5000 N/mm in Y and Z directions — numerically equivalent even though implemented via bushing

expectations.text: Rotational torque functions implement 200 N·mm/deg stiffness (using AX/AY/AZ with /1D conversion, or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Attempts rotational stiffness via bushing rotational_stiffness = 11459.156 (correct conversion: 200 × 180/π), but 'rotational_stiffness' is not a valid bushing parameter in Adams View. GFORCE torque expressions are all 0.0. Neither implementation is functional.

expectations.text: All keywords spelled out fully — no abbreviated commands
expectations.evidence: All command-level keywords fully spelled: defaults units, model create, data_element create spline, part create rigid_body name_and_position, part modify rigid_body mass_properties, marker create, constraint create joint fixed, force create element_like bushing, force create direct general_force

expectations.text: All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
expectations.evidence: All names use full .rubber_mount_model. prefix throughout, e.g. .rubber_mount_model.chassis.mount_j, .rubber_mount_model.subframe.mount_i

expectations.text: No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
expectations.evidence: No adams_id keyword appears anywhere in the CMD file

expectations.text: Mass/inertia set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties, not part create
expectations.evidence: part create rigid_body name_and_position used for location/orientation only; part modify rigid_body mass_properties sets mass=10.0, ixx/iyy/izz=1000.0, ixy/iyz/izx=0.0

expectations.text: center_of_mass_marker NOT passed in any part modify mass_properties call
expectations.evidence: part modify rigid_body mass_properties only specifies mass, ixx, iyy, izz, ixy, iyz, izx — no center_of_mass_marker parameter

expectations.text: CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log (adams_view_validation)
expectations.evidence: Adams View validation FAILED — 'rotational_stiffness' is not a valid parameter for bushing, and 'j_marker_name' is not valid for GFORCE
